questions.description: On January 1st, Eoin places an advertisement in the Irish Times offering… Breach Of Contract.
questions.text: On January 1st, Eoin places an advertisement in the Irish Times offering to sell his car for €3,000 "on a first come first served basis" and providing his postal and his email address. Jenny replies that evening by post saying that she is interested in the car but asking whether Eoin might be prepared to accept a lower price and stating that "€2,750 would seem correct". Although this is the only response Eoin has to his advertisement, he ignores it. On January 14th, Jenny emails Eoin agreeing to pay €3,000. On the same day and prior to reading his emails, Eoin brings the car to an auction run by Cars-or-us Ltd which advertises to potential customers that all cars will be sold "without reserve". Carrie is the only bidder for Eoin's car and she bids €2,000. Eoin instructs the auctioneer to withdraw the car from the auction before the hammer falls. He then sells the car privately to Martin for €2,800. Jenny and Carrie both announce their intention to sue Eoin for breach of contract. Advise Eoin.

questions.description: Frank, a seventeen year old student from Galway has just accepted a place… Negligenc, Breach Of Contract.
questions.text: Frank, a seventeen year old student from Galway has just accepted a place in UCD. He travels by train from Galway to Dublin, his first ever train trip. On his return at the end of the week to Galway, he decides to leave his bag which contains a laptop computer in the cloakroom at the train station in Dublin. The cloakroom is operated by the railway company and is free to customers. He gives the bag to Jill, the cloakroom attendant, and is handed a ticket in return. As he is late for his train, he puts this into his pocket without reading it. Jill carelessly leaves the cloakroom unlocked and unattended while she takes a 30 minute lunch break. On her return she finds that Fran's bag has been stolen. When Frank is notified, he decides to sue the railway company for breach of contract. The railway company does not deny negligence but points to a clause on the back of Frank's ticket and which is also displayed inside the carriages on all their trains. This states that the railway company is not liable for "any act or omission" of the company. Advise Frank as to whether the clause is incorporated into the contract and, if it is, whether it covers the railway company's negligence. Also advise Frank as to the effect, if any, of his age on the contract.

questions.description: Ted runs his own taxi business. Advise Ted.
questions.text: Ted runs his own taxi business. He is approached by Bill, a lawyer, who wants to transport Mr Finny, his goldfish, from a veterinarian clinic in Navan to his house in Dundalk. Bill agrees to pay Ted €100 for the journey and he pays a deposit of €25. When Ted and Mr Finny arrive in Dundalk, Bill claims that he has learned that Ted's licence precludes him from transporting animals (apart from guide-dogs) or fish. He refuses to pay and asks for the return of the deposit. Ted then sells one of his fleet of taxis to Maire. He tells her that the model is in "perfect condition" and is a 2008 model. It subsequently transpires that it is a 2001 model and the roof leaks. It also fails its NCT because it is unroadworthy. Maire claims that both representations made by Ted constitute terms of the contract. Advise Ted.

questions.description: Chuck is employed for three months to canvass for Shane, a politician in the local elections.
questions.text: Chuck is employed for three months to canvass for Shane, a politician in the local elections. One month after he commences work, he becomes embroiled in a heated argument with a member of the public on the topic of bank nationalizations. Chuck throws a punch and is arrested for assault. Despite arguing provocation, he is convicted and sentenced to one month in prison. While in prison, Chuck is approached by Len, a fellow inmate, who is seeking to locate an apartment to live in upon his imminent release. He has heard that Chuck's current tenant has just left. Len is finishing serving a ten year sentence for grievous bodily harm and suggests that it would be in Chuck's "best interests" to lease him the apartment for six months. Chuck reluctantly agrees and a rent is agreed which is 10% above the rate paid by the previous tenant. Upon leaving prison, Chuck phones Shane to tell him that he is ready to complete the last month of his contract but he is informed that the contract is frustrated. Chuck seeks your advice in relation to his contract with Shane and also as to whether the contract with Len is voidable or invalid.

questions.description: Explain the relationship between the doctrines of consideration and promissory estoppel.
questions.text: Explain the relationship between the doctrines of consideration and promissory estoppel.

questions.description: (a) In what circumstances, if any, will damages for mental distress be available for breach of contract?
questions.text: (a) In what circumstances, if any, will damages for mental distress be available for breach of contract? [AND]{.underline} (b) To what extent, if any, is there scope for awarding punitive damages, restitution or an account of profit for breach of contract?

questions.description: (a) The principle of freedom of contract is undermined by the willingness of the judiciary to find implied terms and…
questions.text: (a) The principle of freedom of contract is undermined by the willingness of the judiciary to find implied terms and collateral terms in commercial contracts." Discuss. [OR]{.underline} (b) Explain what is meant by "the doctrine of Privity of contract" and explain the various means by which a third party can enforce a contract.

questions.description: In what circumstances will a unilateral mistake render a contract void for voidable at common law?
questions.text: In what circumstances will a unilateral mistake render a contract void for voidable at common law?

questions.description: Catherine is starting up a catering business and wants to enter into a… Advise Catherine.
questions.text: Catherine is starting up a catering business and wants to enter into a contract for the supply and installation of four new ovens. In order to get the best deal possible, she puts a call for tenders in the local paper. The advertisement states that the lowest or any bid will not necessarily be accepted. Niamh and Seán both send in tenders on time. Niamh's tender states that she can provide and install the ovens for €6,000. Seán's tender states that he can provide and install the ovens for €6,500 "or €500 less than any lower bid". Catherine awards the contract to Seán, on the basis that he will do the work for €5,500. Niamh is furious when she discovers that her tender was unsuccessful, and is threatening to sue Catherine. A month before the catering business is due to open Catherine realises that she needs additional financial support if the business is to succeed. She enters into an agreement with a local food supplier, Thomas, who agrees to invest €10,000 in the business in return for a promise that Catherine will enter into a contract with him for the supply of all foodstuffs to the business in the next two years. However, after two weeks of discussions they fail to agree on important terms of the supply contract, including the price and delivery arrangements. Catherine wants to end negotiations, but Thomas is insisting that she is under contractual duty to negotiate the contract. Advise Catherine.

questions.description: Sinead owns a hairdressing salon in Cork. Breach Of Contract, Penalty Clause.
questions.text: Sinead owns a hairdressing salon in Cork. Last October she decided to expand her business and she hired Pat to build a fully completed extension on to the salon. The contract between Sinead and Pat clearly state that the work was to be finished by January 31st 2011 and a penalty clause stated if the work was not completed by this date that Pat was liable to pay €500 per day until the extension was fully complete. In November, Pat entered into a sub-contract with Charles, a local plumber in the town. Charles agreed to carry out all the plumbing work on the extension by January 15th 2011 at an agreed price of €5,000. At the time of contracting, Pat emphasised that it was essential that this work be done on time, but he did not mention the penalty clause. In mid-December 2010 Charles contacted Pat to say that the work was taking longer than he had anticipated because of the cold weather. He said that he was going to need an extra payment of €2,000 to enable him to hire extra workers so he could finish the job on time. Pat reluctantly agreed to pay the money and Charles managed to finish on time. However once the work was done Pat wrote Charles a letter demanding the return of the additional €2,000 paid, stating that it was not part of the original agreed price and that Charles had "done noting extra for the cash". In addition Sinead is unhappy with the quality of some of Charles 'plumbing work. She claims that the water pressure is too low for washing clients' hair and that there is a major leek from one of the sinks. She is threatening to sue Charles for breach of contract. Advise Charles on his legal position Vis a Vis (a) Pat [and]{.underline} (b) Sinead.

questions.description: Jack is a 70 year old farmer who lives on his own in a rural area in the… Advise Colin.
questions.text: Jack is a 70 year old farmer who lives on his own in a rural area in the west of Ireland. He is in ill health and quite dependent on his neighbour, Sheila, who regularly visits him and assists him with basic household jobs such as cooking and cleaning. Sheila minds Jack's bank account books for him and occasionally drives him to his local bank. One day when Sheila drives Jack to his local bank Jack transfers €20,000 from his personal savings account to Sheila's bank account. Jack does not discuss the transaction in any detail with any bank representative: nor does he receive any independent legal advice on the transaction. Two months later Sheila approaches Jack and asks him to sign a petition to stop the closure of a local school. Jack doesn't have his reading glasses and so he signs the petition without reading it. Jack's nephew Colin visits Jack later that year and upon investigation discovers that the "petition" is, in fact a bank guarantee form which guarantees Sheila's depts. Up to €25,000. Colin is concerned that his uncle may become liable under this guarantee and wants to know if there is any way the guarantee may be void. He also wants to know if the transfer of €20,000 to Sheila can be set aside. Advise Colin.

questions.description: Eamon is the owner of a recording studio. Frustrat, Breach Of Contract.
questions.text: Eamon is the owner of a recording studio. He rents out his recording studio to independent musicians for a fee of €100 per day. On 15th May he agreed to hire out his studio for a 2 week period to Carla. The contract stated that the recording was to take place from 1st June to 14th June and that Carla was to pay a deposit of €500 on 15th May and the balance €900 on the 14th June. Carla paid the deposit on 15th May. However on 31st May, the day before recording was due to begin, Carla got laryngitis and lost her voice. She went to the doctor who told her that she would not be able to sing for at least two weeks. Carla is claiming that the contract is frustrated and that not only does she does not have to pay the €900 due, but also that she is entitled to the return of her €500 deposit. Eamon wants Carla to pay the remaining €900 due especially as he cannot hire out the recording studio to anybody else at such short notice. Eamon also occasionally acts as an agent for up and coming musicians and singers. Three years he agreed to act as an agent for Hannah, a 15 year old singer at the start of her career. The contract stated that Eamon was to receive a commission of 20% of all Hannah's income, from music or related merchandise, for the next 10 years. However, now Hannah is 18 she has decided that she is unhappy with the arrangement with Eamon, and she has told Eamon that she wants to cancel their contract and look for a better deal with another agent. Eamon wants to sue Hannah for breach of contract. Advise Eamon on his legal position Vis a Vis (a) Carla [and]{.underline} (b) Hannah

questions.description: (a) Discuss the duty of a plaintiff to mitigate loss caused by a breach of… Breach Of Contract, Damages.
questions.text: (a) Discuss the duty of a plaintiff to mitigate loss caused by a breach of contract. [And]{.underline} (b) Discuss the difference between a liquidated damages clause and a penalty clause

questions.description: Discuss how exemption clauses are statutorily [and]{.underline} judicially controlled in Ireland
questions.text: Discuss how exemption clauses are statutorily [and]{.underline} judicially controlled in Ireland

questions.description: (a) Discuss the need to reform Irish statutory provisions which provide that certain contracts must be evidenced in…
questions.text: (a) Discuss the need to reform Irish statutory provisions which provide that certain contracts must be evidenced in writing to be enforced [Or]{.underline} (b) Discuss the relationship between the doctrine of consideration and promissory estoppels.

questions.description: Discuss whether [each]{.underline} of the following could be an actionable misrepresentation: (a) A statement of…
questions.text: Discuss whether [each]{.underline} of the following could be an actionable misrepresentation: (a) A statement of intention (b) A statement of opinion (c) Silence
